 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

+$r_{\max} = 4$, hence $S_r = 0$ for $r \geq 5$;
+the degree-$2$ coefficient is

+vanishes on the conformal-weight family; and the charged quartic

+$d_{\mathrm{alg}} \geq 3$ remains.
 \end{proof}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

@@ -950,7 +954,18 @@
 mode pattern~$\nu$, this is exactly the
 Chevalley--Eilenberg differential for the diagonal adjoint action of

+set of mode patterns gives the stated decomposition. More explicitly,
+on the $\nu$-summand every residue bracket has the form
+\[
+x \cdot (a_1 \otimes \cdots \otimes a_n)_\nu
+\;=\;
+\sum_i c_i(\nu)\,
+a_1 \otimes \cdots \otimes [x,a_i] \otimes \cdots \otimes a_n,
+\]
+with coefficients $c_i(\nu)$ depending only on the mode pattern.
+Thus the Lie-theoretic part of $d_1^{\mathrm{PBW}}$ is the
+Chevalley--Eilenberg differential for the diagonal $\fg$-action, not
+for the non-semisimple Lie algebra $\fg \otimes V_h$ itself.
 \end{proof}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

@@ -950,7 +954,18 @@
 mode pattern~$\nu$, this is exactly the
 Chevalley--Eilenberg differential for the diagonal adjoint action of

-set of mode patterns gives the stated decomposition.
+set of mode patterns gives the stated decomposition. More explicitly,
+on the $\nu$-summand every residue bracket has the form
+\[
+x \cdot (a_1 \otimes \cdots \otimes a_n)_\nu
+\;=\;
+\sum_i c_i(\nu)\,
+a_1 \otimes \cdots \otimes [x,a_i] \otimes \cdots \otimes a_n,
+\]
+with coefficients $c_i(\nu)$ depending only on the mode pattern.
+Thus the Lie-theoretic part of $d_1^{\mathrm{PBW}}$ is the
+Chevalley--Eilenberg differential for the diagonal $\fg$-action, not
+for the non-semisimple Lie algebra $\fg \otimes V_h$ itself.
 \end{proof}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

+class-$\mathbf{C}$ witness by itself.}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}
 
 % ================================================================

 succeeded in 50ms:
\begin{conjecture}[Pixton ideal from shadow obstruction towers]
\label{conj:pixton-from-shadows-hgmk}
\ClaimStatusConjectured
\index{Pixton ideal!from shadow obstruction towers|textbf}
\index{shadow obstruction tower!Pixton ideal generation}
For every class~$\mathbf{M}$ chiral algebra $\cA$ with infinite
shadow obstruction tower $\{S_r(\cA)\}_{r \ge 2}$, the MC-descended
tautological relations
$\cR_{g,n}^{\mathrm{MC}}(\cA) \subset R^*(\overline{\cM}_{g,n})$
lie in the Pixton ideal $\cP_{g,n}$. On the standard landscape:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item For rank-$1$ CohFT data \textup{(}the scalar lane\textup{)}:
  this follows from Givental--Teleman reconstruction and the
  Pandharipande--Pixton--Zvonkine theorem
  \textup{(}proved above\textup{)}.

@@ -32493,9 +32586,121 @@
 
 
 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}

+\emph{Global contact witness.}
+The value $d_{\mathrm{alg}} = 2$ is realized by the
+conformal-weight family $\beta\gamma_\lambda$, not by either of its
+one-dimensional internal slices. On the weight-changing line,
+all shadows vanish and $\mu_{\beta\gamma} = 0$ by rank-one abelian
+rigidity

-\textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:betagamma-global-depth}}; Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:contact-stratum-separation}}\textup{)}.
+On the $T$-line, Proposition~\ref{prop:betagamma-T-line-shadows}
+gives $S_3 = 2$, $S_4 = 10/[c(5c{+}22)]$, and an infinite tail.
+The class-$\mathbf{C}$ witness is the full family:
+Theorem~\ref{thm:betagamma-global-depth} gives
+$r_{\max} = 4$, hence $S_r = 0$ for $r \geq 5$;
+the degree-$2$ coefficient is
+$S_2 = 6\lambda^2 - 6\lambda + 1$; the cubic coefficient
+vanishes on the conformal-weight family; and the charged quartic
+is the nonzero constant $S_4 = -5/12$.
+Thus $d_{\mathrm{alg}}(\beta\gamma_\lambda) = 2$.
+The same statement holds for $bc_\lambda$ by the same
+stratum-separation mechanism.

+The classes $\mathbf{G}$, $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and

+and Virasoro/$\mathcal{W}_N$, respectively. No finite value

 % ================================================================
+\subsection{The Verlinde polynomial family}
+\label{subsec:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\index{Verlinde formula!polynomial family}
+
+The Verlinde dimensions $Z_g(k)$ at each genus are
+polynomials in $n = k + 2$ with a universal factorisation
+that reflects the cosecant power sum origin.
+
+\begin{theorem}[Verlinde polynomial family]
+\label{thm:verlinde-polynomial-family}
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere
+For $\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}}_2$ at level $k \geq 1$,
+write $n = k + 2$. The Verlinde dimension
+$Z_g(k) = \sum_{j=0}^{k} S_{0j}^{2-2g}$ is a polynomial
+$P_g(n)$ of degree $3(g-1)$ in $n$ with the following
+structure.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
+\item \textup{(Universal factor.)}
+  For $g \geq 2$,
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-universal-factor}
+    P_g(n) \;=\; n^{g-1}(n^2 - 1) \cdot R_{g-2}(n^2),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $R_{g-2}$ is a polynomial of degree $g - 2$ in
+  $n^2$ with rational coefficients and positive leading
+  term.
+\item \textup{(Explicit formulas through $g = 6$.)}
+  \begin{align}
+    P_2(n) &= \frac{n(n^2 - 1)}{6},
+    \label{eq:P2} \\
+    P_3(n) &= \frac{n^2(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)}{180},
+    \label{eq:P3} \\
+    P_4(n) &= \frac{n^3(n^2 - 1)(2n^4 + 23n^2 + 191)}{7560},
+    \label{eq:P4} \\
+    P_5(n) &= \frac{n^4(n^2 - 1)(n^2 + 11)
+    (3n^4 + 10n^2 + 227)}{226800},
+    \label{eq:P5} \\
+    P_6(n) &= \frac{n^5(n^2 - 1)(2n^8 + 35n^6
+    + 321n^4 + 2125n^2 + 14797)}{2993760}.
+    \label{eq:P6}
+  \end{align}
+\item \textup{(Leading asymptotics.)}
+  The leading coefficient of $P_g(n)$ as $n \to \infty$ is
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-leading}
+    P_g(n) \;\sim\;
+    \frac{\zeta(2g - 2)}{2^{g-2}\,\pi^{2g-2}}\;
+    n^{3(g-1)}
+    \qquad (n \to \infty),
+  \end{equation}
+  where $\zeta$ is the Riemann zeta function.
+\item \textup{(Rational generating function.)}
+  At fixed level $k$ \textup{(}equivalently, fixed $n$\textup{)},
+  the genus generating function
+  \begin{equation}\label{eq:verlinde-gen-fn}
+    G_n(x) \;:=\;
+    \sum_{g \geq 1} Z_g(k)\, x^{g-1}
+    \;=\;
+    \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+    \frac{1}{1 - a_j\, x},
+    \qquad
+    a_j \;=\; \frac{n}{2\sin^2(\pi j/n)},
+  \end{equation}
+  is a rational function of $x$ with $n - 1$ simple poles.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The representation
+$Z_g(k) = (n/2)^{g-1} \sum_{j=1}^{n-1}
+\csc^{2g-2}(\pi j/n)$
+follows from $S_{0j} = \sqrt{2/n} \sin(\pi j/n)$
+(with the re-indexing $j \mapsto j$, $1 \leq j \leq n-1$).
+The cosecant power sum
+$\sigma_m(n) = \sum_{j=1}^{n-1} \csc^{2m}(\pi j/n)$
+is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $2m$ with rational
+coefficients: this is a classical result of Euler,
+with the leading coefficient
+$\sigma_m(n) \sim (2/\pi)^{2m}\,\zeta(2m)\,n^{2m}$.
+Parts~(i)--(iii) follow. For~(iv), the geometric series
+$\sum_{g \geq 1} a_j^{g-1} x^{g-1} = 1/(1 - a_j x)$
+applied term by term to the $j$-sum gives the
+rational generating function.
+The explicit formulas~\eqref{eq:P2}--\eqref{eq:P6}
+are obtained by polynomial interpolation from
+$Z_g(k)$ at $k = 1, \ldots, 3(g-1) + 1$ and verified
+against direct $S$-matrix summation at
+$k = 1, \ldots, 19$.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Structural observations]
+\label{rem:verlinde-poly-structure}
+Three features of the polynomial
+family~\eqref{eq:verlinde-universal-factor} deserve
+comment.
+\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
+\item The factor $n^2 - 1 = (k+1)(k+3)$ vanishes at
+  $k = -1$ and $k = -3$, reflecting the boundary of
+  the integrable range. The factor $n^{g-1}$ vanishes at
+  $n = 0$ ($k = -2$, the critical level), where the
+  Verlinde formula degenerates.
+\item $P_2(n) = \binom{n+1}{3}$ is the unique genus
+  at which $Z_g$ is a single binomial coefficient
+  (the tetrahedral numbers). At $g \geq 3$, the
+  irreducible factor $R_{g-2}(n^2)$ prevents a
+  binomial representation: $(n^2 + 11)$ at $g = 3$
+  has no real roots.
+\item The factor $(n^2 + 11)$ appears in both $P_3$
+  and $P_5$, suggesting a recurrence structure in the
+  polynomials $R_{g-2}$. The denominators
+  $6, 180, 7560, 226800, 2993760$ are
+  $\prod_{j=1}^{g-1}(2j)!/(2j-1)!!$-related, connecting
+  to the Bernoulli number structure of the
+  leading asymptotics~\eqref{eq:verlinde-leading}.
+\end{enumerate}
+\end{remark}
 
-\section{Ordered chiral homology at genus~$2$:
-$\int_{\Sigma_2}^{\mathrm{ord}} Y_\hbar(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$}
